input.background: Substitute the appropriate values of the dummy variables in model 3 to obtain the equations of the three response lines in Figure 12.23.
input.question: What is the equation for the radio medium line?

output.answer: Substitute the appropriate values of $x_2$ and $x_3$ into the expression for $E(y)$ to obtain the radio medium line $\left(x_2=1, x_3=0\right)$: $$\begin{aligned} E(y) & =\beta_0+\beta_1 x_1+\beta_2(1)+\beta_3(0)+\beta_4 x_1(1)+\beta_5 x_1(0) \\ & =\overbrace{\left(\beta_0+\beta_2\right)}^{y \text {-intercept }}+\overbrace{\left(\beta_1+\beta_4\right)}^{\text {Slope }} x_1 \end{aligned}$$

meta_info.caption: Interpreting the $\\beta$ 's in a Model with Mixed Independent Variables
meta_info.section: 12.8 Models with Both Quantitative and Qualitative Variables
